purpose: Realistic JSON fixture files representing the output of each `claude -p` agent call at every pipeline stage. Used by the bats-core test suite so no real Claude calls are made during testing. Fixtures cover Phase 1 (gather findings), developer answers, Phase 4 synthesis passes, and markdown skill/subagent validation cases.

architecture.overview: The directory is organized by pipeline stage and artifact type. Each subdirectory maps to a distinct output category: `findings/` for Phase 1 agent outputs, `synthesis/` for Phase 4 pass outputs, `skills/` and `subagents/` for markdown validation test cases, and two empty placeholder dirs (`envelopes/`, `hooks/`) for future use. Fixtures are plain JSON (or markdown for skill/subagent cases) that tests either copy directly into the work directory or wrap in a Claude API envelope via `make_claude_envelope()` for mock dispatch.

subdirectories.purpose: One JSON file per Phase 1 gather agent. Each file is the `structured_output` field a real `claude -p --output-format json --json-schema` call would return for that agent.

Phase 4 synthesis outputs. output-docs.json is pass 1 (CLAUDE.md generation), output-tooling.json is pass 2 (skills/hooks/MCP), output.json is the merged final artifact ready for Phase 5 validation and write.
Three skill markdown fixtures used exclusively by validate_skill.bats: one valid case and two invalid cases (missing description, angle brackets in description).
Two subagent markdown fixtures used exclusively by validate_subagent.bats: one valid case and one with no frontmatter.
Empty placeholder directory reserved for future pre-built envelope fixtures. Currently envelope wrapping is done inline in tests via make_claude_envelope().
Empty placeholder directory reserved for future hook script fixtures.

architecture.data_flow: Unit tests copy fixture files directly into the temp WORK_DIR (e.g., `cp findings/identity.json $WORK_DIR/findings/`). Integration tests wrap fixtures with `make_claude_envelope()` to produce the full `{is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output}` envelope, then write them to a mock dispatch directory keyed by agent name. The mock `claude` binary reads from the dispatch dir and returns the matching envelope to `run_agent()`, which extracts `.structured_output` into the findings file. Skill/subagent markdown fixtures are passed directly as file paths to the standalone validator scripts.

key_files.purpose: Project metadata output (name, languages, frameworks, monorepo, deployment). Most broadly used fixture — appears in synthesize_helpers.bats, gather_phase.bats, synthesize_phase.bats, and resume.bats.
Complete merged synthesis output used by merge.bats to test merge_settings(), write_mcp_config(), and write_artifacts(). Contains CLAUDE.md + 2 skills + 1 hook + 1 MCP server config + settings_hooks.
Pass 2 synthesis output (skills, hooks, MCP, subagents, settings_hooks). Intentionally lacks claude_md — tests the merge logic that combines pass 1 and pass 2 outputs.
Pass 1 synthesis output (claude_md + subdirectory_claude_mds). Contains a realistic CLAUDE.md with deliberate padding lines to meet minimum length validation thresholds.
Git history analysis output. Has a known field-name mismatch with its schema: uses 'path' in hotspots/coupling arrays where the schema expects 'file'.
Reference example of a fully valid skill — correct YAML frontmatter, kebab-case name, 3+ backtick-wrapped file references, verification section. Used as the positive test case in validate_skill.bats.
Reference example of a valid subagent with YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, allowedTools) and a substantive markdown body.
Simulated Phase 2 developer interview output (project_purpose, deployment_target, never_do, common_mistakes, additional_context). No corresponding schema file exists.

patterns.name: Stage-mapped fixture directories
patterns.description: Each fixture directory corresponds to one pipeline phase output: findings/ → Phase 1, synthesis/ → Phase 4. When adding a new agent, create its fixture at findings/{agent-name}.json. When adding a new synthesis pass, add synthesis/output-{pass-name}.json.

patterns.name: Split synthesis fixtures for merge testing
patterns.description: output-docs.json and output-tooling.json are intentionally separate — they have disjoint top-level keys. This design exercises the merge_synthesis_passes() function. Never pre-merge them manually; output.json is produced by the merge function itself and separately maintained as a fixture for merge phase tests.

patterns.name: Positive + negative validation fixture pairs
patterns.description: Validation test fixtures always come in pairs: one valid case and one or more targeted invalid cases. Each invalid case violates exactly one rule so tests can assert a specific error message. Current pairs: valid-skill + invalid-skill-no-desc + invalid-skill-angle-brackets; valid-subagent + invalid-subagent.

patterns.name: Envelope wrapping for mock dispatch
patterns.description: Integration tests never use fixture JSON raw. They always call make_claude_envelope(content, cost) which produces {is_error: false, total_cost_usd: <n>, structured_output: <content>}. The cost parameter is arbitrary but must be numeric. Synthesis passes use 5.00; agent calls use 0.05.

patterns.name: Minimal but schema-valid content
patterns.description: Each findings fixture contains 1-2 realistic examples per array — just enough to prove the structure is correct and that code doesn't crash on real data. This is intentional: faster tests, easier to read, and edge cases (empty arrays, large arrays) are covered by separate edge/ tests.

conventions.naming: findings fixtures: {agent-name}.json matching the agent's name exactly (e.g., git-forensics.json for the git-forensics agent). Synthesis fixtures: output-{pass}.json for pass outputs, output.json for merged. Skill fixtures: skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md in a directory named after the case. Subagent fixtures: subagents/{name}.md flat.
conventions.imports: No imports — all fixtures are static JSON or markdown. Tests load them via cp, cat, or direct path argument to validator scripts. Envelope wrapping uses make_claude_envelope() from tests/helpers/test_helper.bash.
conventions.error_handling: Invalid fixture cases are structured to fail in exactly one targeted way. Each invalid fixture file name describes the violation (invalid-skill-no-desc, invalid-skill-angle-brackets, invalid-subagent). Tests assert specific error substrings from the validator's stderr/stdout.
other: YAML frontmatter in skill/subagent fixtures must use valid YAML multiline syntax — validators recognize both folded (>) and literal (|) scalars for description fields.
The output.json merged fixture includes explicit padding lines in claude_md to pass the minimum-length validator (100+ lines). Do not remove them.
developer-answers.json has no corresponding schema file in schemas/ — it is freeform JSON with fixed keys.

internal: schemas/ (all .json schema files that findings fixtures must conform to)

external_key.name: bats-core
external_key.purpose: Test runner that loads fixtures in @setup blocks and asserts against them in @test blocks

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: Used in tests to extract fields from fixture JSON for assertions

gotchas.issue: git-forensics.json uses 'path' in hotspots/temporal_coupling/bug_fix_density/ownership_diffusion objects, but the schema (schemas/git-forensics.json) expects 'file'. Also missing 'example_messages' in commit_patterns.
gotchas.solution: Either update the fixture to match the schema field names, or update the schema to match the fixture — whichever reflects what real agents actually produce. Verify against a real claude -p call before changing.

gotchas.issue: tooling.json omits the 'purpose' field that schemas/tooling.json requires for each tool entry.
gotchas.solution: Add a 'purpose' string to each tool object in the fixture, e.g., 'purpose': 'Linting'.

gotchas.issue: output-docs.json and output-tooling.json must NOT be manually merged into output.json — they exist separately to test the merge function. output.json is independently maintained as the expected post-merge result.
gotchas.solution: When updating either pass fixture, also update output.json to match what merge_synthesis_passes() would produce from the new content.

gotchas.issue: Integration tests require envelope wrapping — passing a raw fixture JSON to the mock will cause run_agent() to fail when it calls jq '.structured_output // .result // .' on the response.
gotchas.solution: Always use make_claude_envelope() from test_helper.bash when setting up mock dispatch files.

gotchas.issue: The mock dispatch directory matches fixture files by filename — the file must be named exactly {agent-name}.json. A mismatch silently falls through to MOCK_CLAUDE_RESPONSE, which can mask the mismatch.
gotchas.solution: New agent fixture files must be named identically to the agent's name argument passed to run_agent().

gotchas.issue: The claude_md field in output-docs.json contains deliberate padding to exceed the 100-line minimum for CLAUDE.md validation. Editing the fixture to trim it will cause validate_claude_md tests to fail.
gotchas.solution: If you need to shorten the fixture for readability, keep the padding comment block intact or adjust the test's expected minimum threshold to match.

gotchas.issue: skills/ fixtures use a directory-per-skill layout (skills/{name}/SKILL.md) while subagents/ uses flat files (subagents/{name}.md). These different layouts reflect how validate-skill.sh and validate-subagent.sh expect their input paths.
gotchas.solution: When adding a skill fixture, create a subdirectory. When adding a subagent fixture, place it flat.

gotchas.issue: The envelopes/ and hooks/ directories are empty but intentionally present. Some CI or glob-based test discovery could emit warnings about empty directories.
gotchas.solution: Do not delete them — they are reserved for future fixture types. Add a .gitkeep if your tooling complains.

domain_terms.meaning: Raw JSON output from a Phase 1 gather agent, stored in .ultrainit/findings/{agent-name}.json. Each fixture in findings/ simulates this output.

The wrapper object that real claude -p --output-format json returns: {is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output}. Fixtures contain only the structured_output portion; tests wrap them via make_claude_envelope().

A directory set via MOCK_CLAUDE_DISPATCH_DIR containing per-agent envelope files. The mock claude binary reads the matching file based on the agent name to simulate different responses per agent call.
One of two heavy claude -p calls in Phase 4. Pass 1 produces CLAUDE.md (output-docs.json), pass 2 produces skills/hooks/MCP (output-tooling.json). Both are merged into output.json.
A skill or subagent markdown file used to test validate-skill.sh or validate-subagent.sh. Always comes in valid + targeted-invalid pairs.
The jq path .structured_output within a Claude API envelope — the actual typed JSON payload conforming to the --json-schema passed to claude -p.

skill_opportunities.name: add-agent-fixture
skill_opportunities.description: Scaffolds a new findings fixture for a new gather agent: creates the JSON file from the schema, validates it against the schema, and adds the fixture to the mock dispatch setup in relevant test files.
workflow_steps: Read the agent's schema from schemas/{agent-name}.json
Generate a minimal but realistic fixture JSON conforming to the schema
Cross-check field names match schema (especially string vs enum fields)
Find all test files that set up dispatch directories and add the new fixture there
